← the Atlas

ChromeBar declutter — collapse the rail when it agrees

feature · budding ·implemented ·JUN 14, 2026

The srv·client·kaval identity rail prints the same commit three times in the happy path. Collapse it to one calm health-dot + version + shared SHA at rest, and fan back out to per-source detail only when a source actually diverges — so every alarm stays loud and nothing becomes unreachable.

Contents

  • The crowding — three SHAs for one bit
  • Collapse when they agree
  • Fan out on divergence
  • What moves where — nothing is lost
  • Alternatives weighed

The rail is too crowded — and most of it is the same fact, repeated. In a clean deploy the server, this browser’s bundle, and the kaval daemon are all built from one HEAD, so the rail prints d07ea54 three times behind three labels and two dividers. packages/client/src/ui/IdentityRail.tsx Shipped in #1359 .

The crowding — three SHAs for one bit

srvv1.1.0d07ea54clientd07ea54kavald07ea5498eeac01h 50m
Today — d07ea54 printed three times (highlighted), two dots, two dividers, three labels: ~10 tokens to say in sync · alive.

The three columns exist to flag disagreement — client ≠ srv (a stale cached bundle), kaval ⬆ update (the daemon a build behind). When everything agrees — the ~95% case — that machinery just repeats one identity. Only 98eeac0 (kaval’s nix closure hash) is genuinely distinct.

This revisits a decision we deliberately deferred

The original rail chose “always two explicit columns… no collapse-when-equal” — but only “until the feature stabilizes” (pty-daemon-chrome-bar, decisions). Since then B2 split kaval into its own daemon, B3.2 added the inline restart, and B3.4 ( #1353 ) lit the ⬆ update nudge. The feature has stabilized — so collapse-when-equal is now on the table.

Collapse when they agree

At rest, one calm thing: a single worst-of health dot, the version, and the shared commit shown once. Hover (or click the ▸) for the full three-way split — nothing is lost, it just stops shouting.

v1.1.0d07ea54▸
srvopenv1.1.0d07ea54
clientfreshd07ea54
kavalrunning1h 50md07ea54 · 98eeac0
Calm mode — one dot (worst-of ws · client · kaval) + version + one SHA. The ▸ opens KavalInfoDialog; the card above is the hover <Tip>.

The whole switch rides accessors that already exist — no new derivation, no wire change:

// the resting dot: worst-of, reusing the existing wsDot / kavalDot tables.
// Branch order IS the priority: a down link wins (identity is untrustworthy
// with no server to read), then an unknown daemon stays grey — never a
// false-green before the first status yield — then a down/warming daemon,
// then the amber currency nudge, else all-clear.
const unifiedDot = (): string => {
  if (props.status !== "open") return wsDot(props.status);
  const state = daemon()?.state;
  if (!state) return kavalDot(undefined);           // grey-not-red trap kept
  if (downState() || daemonWarming()) return kavalDot(state);
  if (stale() || kavalUpdatePending()) return nudgeDot;
  return toneDot.ok;
};

// No aggregate `diverged()` predicate: each source decides for itself whether
// to fan back out, via its own <Show>. The kaval source is the one that needs
// a named test (behind, or not cleanly running); ws (props.status !== "open")
// and client (stale()) are <Show> guards inline.
const kavalDiverged = (): boolean =>
  kavalUpdatePending() || !!downState() || daemonWarming();

Fan out on divergence

The collapse is a steady-state optimization, never an alarm muffler. The moment any axis goes non-green, the offending source re-materializes inline with its own dot, SHA and actionable chip — louder than today, not quieter. WS-down and daemon-down stay distinct reds (server-unreachable ≠ daemon-dead).

client behind server

v1.1.0d07ea54cliente1f2a3c≠ srv▸
amber dot · the client row fans back out with its own SHA — one-click reload. clientStale()

kaval a build behind

v1.1.0d07ea54kaval98eeac01h 50m⬆ update▸
amber dot · click ▸ → dialog (where Restart-kaval lives). kavalUpdatePending()

daemon stopped

v1.1.0d07ea54kavalnot running▸
red dot, surfaced as the kaval source. downState() · pairs with the degraded canvas.

server unreachable

v1.1.0srvoffline
red dot surfaced as srv — a different red from a dead daemon. props.status

What moves where — nothing is lost

Current elementSource accessorNew home
srv / client / kaval labelsliteralTooltip rows (off the resting strip)
WS dot · kaval dotwsDot · kavalDotOne worst-of dot at rest; the specific dot surfaces on divergence
version v1.1.0server()?.versionInline, kept — cheap, identifying, not redundant
server commitserver()?.commitInline as the one shared SHA + Tip row
client commitclientCommitTip row; inline beside ≠ srv when stale; also About dialog
kaval commitidentity?.navigableCommitTip row + dialog (already there)
closure 98eeac0identity?.staleKeyOff the strip → Tip row + dialog (.slice(0,12))
uptime 1h 50mformatUptime(...)Tip row + dialog — reference detail, not a glance signal
≠ srv · ⬆ updateclientStale() · kavalUpdatePending()Inline, unchanged — they fire the dot + the fan-out

Scope — one component, ~half a day

Almost entirely a re-layout of packages/client/src/ui/IdentityRail.tsx: collapse the three &lt;span&gt; columns into one &lt;Tip&gt;-wrapped row, &lt;Show&gt;-gate the fan-out. KavalInfoDialog already holds the deep store — no change. Two traps: keep routing the unknown daemon through kavalDot(undefined) (grey, never a false red — #1034); and keep the data-ws-status / data-daemon-state attributes on whatever dot now carries each axis, so e2e selectors don’t break.

Alternatives weighed

Lighter and heavier options, for overriding toward conservative. The judged verdict: each attacks the wrong axis or muffles an alarm.

A · dedupe only

v1.1.0d07ea54
Drop labels + dup SHAs, keep 3 dots. ~30 min change — but still three dots to decode. partial

B · dialog-only

v1.1.0▸
Everything behind the chevron. Maximal calm — but an alarm behind a click a calm user never makes. rejected

C · one dot, three SHAs

v1.1.0d07ea54·d07ea54·d07ea54
Merge the dots, keep the SHAs. Leaves the actual redundancy untouched. wrong axis

D · icon-segmented

⌑▢◆d07ea54
Glyph per source. Trades text-clutter for an icon-decode puzzle. rejected

The recommended unified-identity / fan-out shape is the only one that hits both axes at once: it kills the triple-SHA redundancy and keeps every alarm inline and loud — divergence is exactly what the rail was built to show.
